title: Real estate and US reindustrialisation: Savills' Ken Biberaj
gist: A Benchmark Minerals Giga US 2026 video listing page featuring Ken Biberaj of Savills arguing that land, power, and permitting — not just federal policy — are the binding real estate constraints on US reindustrialisation; surrounding episodes span deep sea mining policy, LFP manufacturing flexibility, critical mineral supply chain investment, and EV demand drivers.

themes.theme: Real Estate as Binding Constraint on US Reindustrialisation

insights.claim: US reindustrialisation depends not just on trade and industrial policy but on physical real estate availability — land, power access, and permitting — which Savills' Ken Biberaj argues are the binding constraints that can strand manufacturing and critical mineral projects regardless of federal intent.
insights.speaker: Ken Biberaj
insights.so_what: Industrial real estate — specifically industrial land with grid access and permitting pathways — is a scarce input that investors in US manufacturing and critical minerals projects must underwrite alongside policy risk.
insights.quote: US reindustrialisation depends not just on policy, but on the availability of land, power and permitting
insights.locator: Real estate and US reindustrialisation: Interview with Savills' Ken Biberaj

insights.claim: Industrial policy for US reindustrialisation must bridge the gap between federal ambition and local execution — without that connection, Washington's commitments to onshoring manufacturing will not translate into operating facilities.
insights.speaker: Ken Biberaj
insights.so_what: Investors in US manufacturing projects should treat local permitting and community/utility engagement as critical path items, not post-funding formalities.
insights.quote: industrial policy must connect federal ambition with local execution
insights.locator: real estate constraints shaping critical minerals and manufacturing projects

themes.theme: Critical Minerals Policy Landscape — Giga US 2026

insights.claim: US deep sea mining commercial development is gated primarily by policy clarity on permitting, regulation, and the legal framework — not technology or resource availability — according to The Metals Company's Craig Shesky in a parallel Giga US 2026 interview.
insights.speaker: Craig Shesky
insights.so_what: Watch US regulatory developments on deep sea mining closely; any policy clarity event is a potential catalyst for The Metals Company and peers.
insights.locator: US deep sea mining policy: Interview with The Metals Company's Craig Shesky

insights.claim: As US energy storage demand accelerates, manufacturing flexibility — specifically the capacity to shift production toward LFP (lithium iron phosphate) chemistry — is becoming a competitive differentiator for battery manufacturers such as LG Energy Solution.
insights.speaker: Bob Lee
insights.so_what: LFP capacity flexibility is emerging as a strategic moat in US battery manufacturing; investors should assess which players have chemically flexible production lines versus locked-in NMC configurations.
insights.quote: manufacturing flexibility is becoming a competitive advantage
insights.locator: US energy storage policy and manufacturing: Interview with LG Energy Solution's Bob Lee

insights.claim: Policy certainty is framed as a prerequisite for unlocking further domestic battery manufacturing investment by LG Energy Solution — suggesting battery makers are waiting on regulatory signals before committing to LFP scale-up in the US.
insights.speaker: Bob Lee
insights.so_what: Policy delays on domestic content rules and IRA battery guidance extend the investment overhang for US battery gigafactory build-out.
insights.quote: the policy certainty needed to unlock further domestic investment
insights.locator: US energy storage policy and manufacturing: Interview with LG Energy Solution's Bob Lee

insights.claim: Investment in critical mineral supply chains was framed at Giga US 2026 as essential to both economic competitiveness and national security — a dual framing that has historically broadened bipartisan political support and justified higher public funding thresholds.
insights.speaker: Benchmark Minerals
insights.so_what: National security framing of critical minerals is durable across administrations; it supports sustained federal funding and offtake commitments that underpin junior miner valuations.
insights.quote: Accelerating investment in critical mineral supply chains crucial for economic competitiveness and national security
insights.locator: Investment Panel: Accelerating investment in critical mineral supply chains

insights.claim: Software and autonomy tools are identified by Mariana Minerals CEO Turner Caldwell as actionable levers to improve efficiency and lower costs in domestic US copper and lithium projects, positioning technology adoption as a path to cost-competitive domestic supply.
insights.speaker: Turner Caldwell
insights.so_what: Mining software and autonomy represent an investable sub-theme within the critical minerals supply chain; companies enabling lower-cost domestic production via software are structurally advantaged.
insights.quote: how software can improve efficiency, lower costs and strengthen domestic supply chains
insights.locator: Advancing US critical minerals with software and autonomy: Interview with Mariana Minerals' Turner Caldwell

insights.claim: Australia and Brazil are highlighted as growing critical materials suppliers at Giga US 2026, indicating that supply chain diversification away from Chinese-dominated processing is proceeding across multiple geographies simultaneously.
insights.speaker: Benchmark Minerals
insights.so_what: Australian and Brazilian critical mineral projects benefit from sustained investor and policy tailwinds as Western supply chains diversify; watch for offtake deals with US manufacturers.
insights.locator: Regional Focus Presentation: The growing role of Australian critical materials

source_meta.author: Ken Biberaj, Executive Managing Director, Savills; Bryan Bille, Lead Policy and Geopolitical Analyst, Benchmark
